questions.question_text:
['Example — Treatment 3: (0.3, 0.9) vs (0.9, 0.3)]

Option 1:
  Box 1 (if you WON): {{ option1_box1_red }} red balls, {{ option1_box1_black }} black balls
  Box 2 (if you LOST): {{ option1_box2_red }} red balls, {{ option1_box2_black }} black balls
  Overall: {{ option1_overall_red_pct }} percent red, {{ option1_overall_black_pct }} percent black
  If red ball drawn: {{ option1_posterior_red_pct }}% chance you won | If black ball drawn: {{ option1_posterior_black_pct }}% chance you won

Option 2:
  Box 1 (if you WON): {{ option2_box1_red }} red balls, {{ option2_box1_black }} black balls
  Box 2 (if you LOST): {{ option2_box2_red }} red balls, {{ option2_box2_black }} black balls
  Overall: {{ option2_overall_red_pct }} percent red, {{ option2_overall_black_pct }} percent black
  If red ball drawn: {{ option2_posterior_red_pct }}% chance you won | If black ball drawn: {{ option2_posterior_black_pct }}% chance you won

Which option do you prefer? You must choose one option even if one of them provides no information.

After choosing, you will indicate your preference strength on a scale from 0 (completely indifferent) to 10 (extremely strong preference for chosen option). You will also be asked: what is the minimum amount in cents (between 1 and 50 cents) you would need to receive to switch from your preferred option to the other option?
```']

After choosing, you will indicate your preference strength on a scale from 0 (completely indifferent) to 10 (extremely strong preference for chosen option). You will also be asked: what is the minimum amount in cents (between 1 and 50 cents) you would need to receive to switch from your preferred option to the other option?

option_labels.10: extremely strong preference for chosen option
